name: 5-Note Scale (1-2-3-4-5-4-3-2-1)
description: The classic warm-up. Sing up 5 notes of the major scale and back down. Focus on even tone and smooth transitions between notes.
goodFor: Building even tone across your range and warming up your voice for any practice session. A go-to daily exercise for singers at every level.

name: Major Triad (1-3-5-3-1)
description: Sing each note of the major triad ascending and descending. Focus on clean pitch and even tone across the intervals.
goodFor: Training your ear to lock into chord tones and developing clean intonation across wider intervals. Strengthens your harmonic foundation.

name: Octave Arpeggio (1-3-5-8-5-3-1)
description: Full octave arpeggio. Great for building range and bridging registers. Keep your support steady through the top note.
goodFor: Bridging between chest and head voice while building range. Teaches you to maintain support through wide intervals and register transitions.

name: Chromatic Scale (5 Half Steps)
description: Chromatic half-step exercise. Excellent for pitch accuracy and ear training. Keep each note clean and distinct.
goodFor: Sharpening pitch accuracy and ear training. The half-step movement demands precision and builds the muscle memory for hitting notes dead-center.

name: Fifth Interval Jump (1-5-1-5-1)
description: Practice jumping a perfect fifth. Focus on nailing the interval without scooping. Builds interval recognition.
goodFor: Developing interval recognition and clean pitch on one of the most common jumps in music. Builds confidence for melodic leaps in songs.

name: Major Scale (Full Octave)
description: Full major scale up and down one octave. The foundation of Western music. Focus on even spacing and consistent tone.
goodFor: Establishing solid intonation across a full octave and developing even tone throughout your range. The backbone of all Western vocal music.

name: Minor Triad (1-b3-5-b3-1)
description: Minor triad arpeggio. Practice the darker tonality with clean pitch. Notice the difference from the major triad.
goodFor: Training your ear for minor tonality and building comfort with darker harmonic colors. Essential for emotive singing in ballads and contemporary styles.

name: Dominant 7th Arpeggio (1-3-5-b7-5-3-1)
description: Dominant seventh chord arpeggio. Adds the flat-7th for a bluesy, jazzy quality. Great for genre versatility.
goodFor: Expanding your harmonic vocabulary and developing comfort with jazz and blues intervals. Adds genre versatility to your singing.

name: Staccato Repeated Note
description: Repeat the same note with short, bouncy staccato. Builds diaphragm control and clean onset. Think 'ha ha ha ha ha'.
goodFor: Building diaphragm control, clean vocal onsets, and breath support. The short, bouncy attacks train the core muscles of singing.

name: Sustained Single Tone
description: Hold a single note as long and steady as possible. Focus on consistent airflow, no wobble. Build your breath support foundation.
goodFor: Developing breath control, steady airflow, and consistent tone. The simplest yet most revealing exercise — exposes any weakness in your support.

name: Ascending Thirds
description: Weaving thirds pattern. Builds agility and interval awareness. Keep it smooth and connected.
goodFor: Building vocal agility and interval awareness with weaving patterns. Prepares you for the kind of melodic movement found in real songs.

name: Octave Jump (1-8-1)
description: Jump a full octave and back. The ultimate interval challenge. Keep both notes in tune without scooping.
goodFor: Mastering the widest common interval and building confidence across your full range. Teaches clean pitch without scooping on dramatic leaps.

name: Descending 5-Note Scale
description: Start from the 5th and descend. Great for practicing head-to-chest transitions. Keep the tone consistent as you come down.
goodFor: Practicing smooth head-to-chest voice transitions and training a consistent descending tone. Great for singers who tend to lose support on the way down.

name: Lip Trill / Bubble Scale
description: Do this exercise on a lip trill (bubble lips). Relaxes tension, warms up the voice gently. Perfect first exercise of any session.
goodFor: Releasing jaw and lip tension while gently warming up your voice. The back-pressure from the bubbling balances airflow and cord closure naturally.

name: Pentatonic Scale
description: The 5-note pentatonic scale used in pop, R&B, and rock. No awkward half steps. Great for riff building.
goodFor: Building familiarity with the five-note scale used in pop, R&B, rock, and country. The foundation for learning riffs and improvisation.

name: Natural Minor Scale (Full Octave)
description: Full natural minor scale. Practice the darker, more emotional tonality. Essential for classical and contemporary styles.
goodFor: Developing comfort with minor tonality across a full octave. Essential for classical repertoire, emotional ballads, and contemporary styles.

name: Messa di Voce (Swell)
description: Hold a single note. Start quiet, crescendo to full volume, then diminuendo back to quiet. The ultimate dynamic control exercise.
goodFor: Mastering dynamic control — the ability to go from quiet to loud and back on a single note. One of the most important skills for expressive singing.

name: Rapid 5-Note Scale
description: The classic 5-note scale at double speed. Builds vocal agility and coordination. Don't sacrifice pitch for speed.
goodFor: Building vocal speed and coordination without sacrificing pitch accuracy. Develops the agility needed for fast passages in songs.

name: Siren Slide (1 to 8)
description: Glide smoothly from root to octave like a siren. Focus on seamless register transitions. Don't break the sound.
goodFor: Smoothing out register transitions and exploring your full range. The continuous glide helps you find and eliminate breaks between chest and head voice.

name: Fourth Interval Jump (1-4-1-4-1)
description: Practice the perfect fourth interval. A natural-sounding jump found everywhere in music. Keep both notes centered.
goodFor: Training one of music's most natural-sounding intervals. Builds interval recognition and clean pitch on a jump found constantly in melodies.

name: Two-Octave Arpeggio
description: Extended two-octave arpeggio for advanced range work. Challenges your full register from chest through head voice.
goodFor: Challenging your full vocal range from low chest through high head voice. An advanced exercise for singers ready to push their limits safely.

name: Diminished 7th Arpeggio
description: Each interval is a minor third. The symmetrical pattern trains your ear for unusual intervals common in classical and jazz.
goodFor: Training your ear for symmetrical, unusual intervals common in classical and jazz. Builds pitch accuracy on intervals that don't fall naturally.

name: Tongue Trill Scale
description: Rolled R (tongue trill) through the 5-note scale. An alternative to lip trills that works the same relaxation benefits with different coordination.
goodFor: Warming up with gentle tongue engagement and releasing tension. An alternative to lip trills that adds coordination training for the tongue.

name: Humming Scale
description: Hum with closed lips through the 5-note scale. Feel the vibrations in your face (mask resonance). The gentlest possible warm-up.
goodFor: The gentlest possible warm-up — building mask resonance, forward placement, and awareness of facial vibrations without any strain on your voice.

name: Nay Twang Exercise
description: Sing 'Nay' with an exaggerated twang. Builds forward placement and bright resonance. Essential for contemporary singing and belt technique.
goodFor: Developing forward placement, bright resonance, and the twang quality essential for belting. A cornerstone exercise for pop, musical theater, and country singers.

name: Vocal Fry to Chest Voice
description: Start in vocal fry, then gradually add air to transition into full chest voice. Builds cord closure and clean onset.
goodFor: Building clean cord closure and learning to start notes without a breathy or glottal attack. Trains the foundation of healthy vocal onset.

name: Yawn-Sigh
description: Start high and sigh downward through your full range. Releases tension and helps find relaxed head voice. Think of a lazy yawn.
goodFor: Releasing tension in the throat and jaw while finding relaxed head voice. A natural, feel-good exercise that resets your voice when it's tired or tight.

name: Descending Staccato 5-Note
description: Staccato bouncing from the 5th descending. Builds agility and diaphragmatic engagement with descending patterns.
goodFor: Developing agility and diaphragmatic engagement on descending patterns. Builds the control needed for quick, accurate downward runs.

name: Wide Arpeggio (1-5-8-5-1)
description: Skips the third and jumps straight to the fifth and octave. Challenging intervals that build range and intonation simultaneously.
goodFor: Building range and intonation simultaneously with challenging interval skips. Teaches you to stay supported through wide melodic leaps.

name: Semitone Trill
description: Rapidly alternate between two adjacent notes. Builds laryngeal flexibility and vocal agility. Start slow and increase speed.
goodFor: Developing laryngeal flexibility and speed for ornaments, trills, and fast passages. An advanced agility builder that unlocks vocal decoration.

name: Blues Scale
description: The classic blues scale with the blue note (b5). Essential for gospel, R&B, blues, and rock. Feel the soul in those chromatic passing tones.
goodFor: Adding soul and style to your singing with the classic blues sound. Essential for gospel, R&B, blues, and rock improvisation and riffing.

name: Major 6th Interval Jump
description: Jump a major sixth — the opening interval of 'My Bonnie Lies Over the Ocean.' A wide, beautiful interval that challenges intonation.
goodFor: Mastering a wide, expressive interval that challenges your intonation. Builds confidence for the dramatic leaps found in Broadway and classical repertoire.

name: 9-Note Rapid Run
description: A blazing fast run through 9 notes and beyond. The ultimate agility test. Speed comes from relaxation, not force.
goodFor: The ultimate agility test — building speed, accuracy, and relaxation through extended fast passages. For singers ready to execute impressive vocal runs.

name: Vowel Modification (Ah-Eh-Ee-Oh-Oo)
description: Hold a single pitch while cycling through all five Italian vowels. Trains vowel purity and consistent resonance across different mouth shapes.
goodFor: Training pure vowel shapes and consistent resonance. Essential for singing clearly in any language and maintaining tone quality across different vowel sounds.

name: Sob / Cry Exercise
description: Sing with a gentle 'crying' quality. This tilts the larynx forward, thins the vocal folds, and helps access head voice with emotion.
goodFor: Accessing head voice with emotion and warmth. The cry quality tilts the larynx forward, thins the folds, and helps you sing high without strain.

name: Belt Arpeggio (1-3-5-8)
description: Ascend-only arpeggio finishing on the octave. Build toward holding the top note with chest-dominant mix. The money note exercise.
goodFor: Building chest-dominant mix and belt power for high notes. The money-note exercise for musical theater, pop, and any style that demands powerful high notes.

name: Mum Scale (Mix Bridge)
description: Sing 'Mum' through a full octave scale. The nasal M consonant helps bridge chest and head voice smoothly through the passaggio.
goodFor: Smoothly bridging chest and head voice through the passaggio. The nasal M consonant helps you navigate the break without flipping or cracking.

name: Bratty Nay Octave (1-8-1)
description: Sing 'Nay' with a bratty, nasal quality on an octave jump. The twang keeps mix voice engaged through the break. Embrace the ugly sound.
goodFor: Training mix voice engagement through a wide interval. The bratty quality keeps cord closure through the passaggio — essential for eliminating the vocal break.

name: Mix Voice Slide (1 to 10)
description: Slide smoothly from chest voice up through the passaggio into head voice. The goal is a seamless, connected sound with no break or flip.
goodFor: Finding and developing the seamless blend between chest and head voice. Teaches you to navigate your passaggio without a break, flip, or sudden shift in tone.

name: Gee Scale (Mix Voice)
description: Sing 'Gee' through the 5-note scale. The G consonant creates cord compression that helps maintain chest voice quality as you ascend into mix.
goodFor: Maintaining chest voice quality as you ascend into mix. The G consonant creates the cord compression needed to keep your sound strong and connected going up.

name: Hooty Oo Scale
description: Sing a 5-note scale on a hooty 'oo' vowel. The round, owl-like vowel naturally engages head voice resonance without strain.
goodFor: Engaging head voice resonance in a gentle, strain-free way. The round vowel naturally places your voice in the right spot for easy upper-range singing.

name: Falsetto Slide Down
description: Start in light falsetto at the top and glide down through your range. Builds control and awareness of your upper register. Keep it light.
goodFor: Building awareness and control of your upper register. The light, descending glide develops falsetto strength and smooths the transition back to chest voice.

name: Head Voice Arpeggio (light)
description: Sing the octave arpeggio with a light, heady tone. Focus on keeping the sound forward and resonant without adding chest weight.
goodFor: Strengthening head voice while maintaining forward resonance. Teaches you to sing high notes that are light but full, not breathy or thin.

name: Ee Head Voice Scale
description: Full octave scale on 'ee' vowel. The narrow vowel naturally encourages head voice placement. Keep it light and bright, no pushing.
goodFor: Developing head voice placement with a vowel that naturally encourages it. The narrow Ee guides your voice into the right resonance for your upper range.

name: Vibrato Pulses
description: Hold a note and pulse the vibrato intentionally. Think of gently bouncing on the note with your diaphragm. Builds muscle memory for natural vibrato.
goodFor: Building the muscle memory for natural vibrato through intentional diaphragmatic pulsing. Teaches your body the oscillation pattern that eventually becomes automatic.

name: Sustained Vibrato Hold
description: Hold a single note and let vibrato develop naturally. Start straight, then allow oscillation to emerge. The goal is even, consistent vibrato.
goodFor: Developing even, consistent vibrato that adds warmth and beauty to sustained notes. The goal is natural oscillation that emerges from relaxed, supported singing.

name: Vibrato Semitone Oscillation
description: Slowly alternate between two adjacent notes to mimic the vibrato motion. Gradually speed up over time. Trains the laryngeal flexibility needed for natural vibrato.
goodFor: Training the laryngeal flexibility needed for natural vibrato by slowly mimicking the oscillation. A mechanical approach that builds the physical coordination over time.

name: Diaphragm Bounce
description: Repeated notes with deliberate diaphragmatic pulsing. Think 'huh huh huh.' Builds the breath support foundation that enables natural vibrato.
goodFor: Strengthening the diaphragmatic engagement that underlies vibrato, staccato, and all breath-based vocal techniques. The physical foundation of advanced singing.

name: Fast Pentatonic Run
description: Speed through the pentatonic scale up and back. The foundation of pop, R&B, and gospel riffs. Start slow, build up speed over weeks.
goodFor: Building speed on the pentatonic scale — the foundation of pop, R&B, and gospel riffs. Develops the agility to execute impressive vocal runs.

name: Three-Note Turn
description: A classic ornament: step up, return, step up again. This weaving pattern is the building block of every vocal run and riff. Master this first.
goodFor: Mastering the fundamental ornament that builds into every vocal run and riff. Once you nail this pattern, complex runs become combinations of this basic move.

name: Descending Riff Cascade
description: Cascade down through scale tones in groups. The classic R&B and gospel descending run. Think Beyonce, Whitney, Mariah.
goodFor: Executing the classic R&B and gospel descending run. Builds the speed and accuracy needed for the kind of vocal decoration heard from the greatest singers.

name: Gospel Run
description: An extended fast run combining scales and a leap to the octave. The full gospel/R&B run experience. Speed comes from relaxation, not force.
goodFor: Performing extended, impressive vocal runs combining scales and leaps. The full gospel and R&B run experience for singers ready to show off their agility.

name: Straw Phonation Scale
description: Sing through a straw (or mimic the narrowing) on a 5-note scale. The gold standard of modern voice therapy. Balances airflow and vocal fold vibration effortlessly.
goodFor: Balancing airflow and vocal fold vibration with the gold standard of semi-occluded vocal tract exercises. Used by professional singers and voice therapists worldwide.

name: Sustained Vvv Buzz
description: Hold a voiced 'Vvv' fricative on a single pitch. The lip contact creates back-pressure that gently massages the vocal folds. A powerful SOVT exercise.
goodFor: Gently massaging the vocal folds through back-pressure created by the voiced fricative. A therapeutic exercise that promotes healthy phonation and reduces strain.

name: Sustained Zzz Buzz Scale
description: Sing 'Zzz' through a 5-note scale. The voiced fricative activates forward resonance and creates gentle back-pressure for healthy phonation.
goodFor: Activating forward resonance and healthy phonation through voiced fricative back-pressure. A versatile SOVT exercise that warms up your voice safely.

name: Ma Me Mi Mo Mu Scale
description: Each ascending note gets a different vowel: Ma-Me-Mi-Mo-Mu. Trains smooth vowel transitions while maintaining forward placement through the nasal M consonant.
goodFor: Training smooth transitions between vowel shapes while maintaining forward placement. Builds the consistency needed for clear, connected singing across all vowels.

name: Bah Onset Exercise
description: Sing 'Bah' on repeated notes. The bilabial plosive B builds clean cord closure without glottal attack. One of the most universal onset exercises in vocal pedagogy.
goodFor: Developing clean, healthy vocal onsets without glottal attack. The B consonant builds cord closure naturally, making this one of the most universal onset exercises.

name: Vee Resonance Scale
description: Sing 'Vee' through the 5-note scale. The labiodental V activates forward resonance and mask placement. Used by speech therapists and vocal coaches alike.
goodFor: Activating forward resonance and mask placement for a brighter, more projected tone. Combines SOVT benefits with resonance training in one exercise.

name: Wah Open Throat Scale
description: Sing 'Wah' through the 5-note scale. The W glide naturally opens the throat while the Ah vowel encourages relaxed, open resonance. Great for releasing tension.
goodFor: Releasing throat tension and encouraging relaxed, open resonance. The W glide naturally opens the throat while the Ah vowel promotes a free, unrestricted sound.

name: Gentle Descending Hum
description: Softly hum from the 5th down to the root. A gentle cool-down that releases tension and brings the voice back to a relaxed state after intensive practice.
goodFor: Winding down your voice gently after practice. The soft descending hum releases remaining tension and brings your vocal folds back to a relaxed state.

name: Low Range Sigh
description: A gentle chromatic sigh from octave down to root. Like the yawn-sigh but specifically for cooling down. Keep it light, breathy, and completely tension-free.
goodFor: Cooling down with a gentle chromatic descent that keeps everything light and tension-free. Specifically designed to release strain after intensive upper-range work.

name: Cool-Down Lip Trill
description: Gentle descending lip trill to wind down your voice. The bubbling releases any remaining tension in the lips and jaw. Use minimal effort.
goodFor: Releasing jaw and lip tension after singing. The gentle bubbling motion relaxes the facial muscles while the descending pattern brings your voice to rest.

name: Quiet Sustained Oo
description: Hold a very quiet 'Oo' on a comfortable pitch. Pianissimo singing is the perfect cool-down — it requires minimal cord contact and gentle airflow. End your session here.
goodFor: The perfect final exercise of any session. Pianissimo singing requires minimal cord contact and gentle airflow, bringing your voice to complete rest.

name: Chest Voice Power Scale
description: Sing 'Hey' with strong chest voice resonance on the 5-note scale. Stay in your lower range and focus on a full, grounded sound. Build the foundation of your voice.
goodFor: Building a strong, resonant chest voice foundation. Develops the grounded, powerful lower register that anchors your entire vocal range.

name: Hey Call Exercise
description: Call out 'Hey!' on the root then the fifth. Like calling across a room. Builds projection, forward placement, and chest voice power through speech-like sounds.
goodFor: Developing projection, forward placement, and natural chest voice power through speech-like sounds. Builds the confident, carrying tone needed for live performance.

name: Minor 3rd Interval Jump (1-b3-1-b3-1)
description: Jump a minor third — the soulful, bluesy interval. Think of the first two notes of 'Greensleeves.' Trains your ear for this essential interval.
goodFor: Training your ear for one of music's most soulful intervals. Found in blues, R&B, and folk melodies — essential for emotive, genre-authentic singing.

name: Major 3rd Interval Jump (1-3-1-3-1)
description: Jump a major third — the bright, happy interval. Think of the first two notes of 'When the Saints Go Marching In.' Clean and precise.
goodFor: Mastering the bright, optimistic interval heard in countless melodies. Builds clean pitch accuracy on this fundamental harmonic building block.

name: Minor 7th Interval Jump (1-b7-1-b7-1)
description: Jump a minor seventh — a wide, dramatic interval. Think of the opening of 'Somewhere' from West Side Story. Challenges your range and intonation.
goodFor: Tackling a wide, dramatic interval that challenges both range and intonation. Builds confidence for the expressive leaps in musical theater and jazz.

name: Pa-Ta-Ka Articulation
description: Alternate Pa-Ta-Ka on repeated notes. Each consonant activates different articulators: Pa (lips), Ta (tongue tip), Ka (tongue back). The gold standard of diction exercises.
goodFor: The gold standard diction exercise — each consonant activates different articulators for complete mouth coordination. Essential for clear lyrics in any genre.

name: Tongue Twister Scale
description: Sing 'Bri-Bri-Bri' or 'Dri-Dri-Dri' through the 5-note scale. Consonant clusters force precise tongue coordination at speed. Essential for clear diction while singing.
goodFor: Building precise tongue coordination at speed for clear diction while singing. Consonant clusters force your articulators to work independently and accurately.

name: Rapid Consonant Switch
description: Alternate 'Bee-Dee-Gee' rapidly through the scale. Each consonant shifts the point of articulation forward to back. Builds independent control of lips, tongue tip, and tongue back.
goodFor: Developing independent control of lips, tongue tip, and tongue back. Builds the articulator separation needed for crisp consonants in fast-moving lyrics.

name: Legato Phrase Exercise
description: Sing 'La' in a long, connected phrase over an octave and back. Focus on seamless legato — no breaks, no bumps, just a smooth line of sound from start to finish.
goodFor: Mastering smooth, connected singing across a wide range. Teaches you to create one seamless line of sound — the essence of beautiful vocal phrasing.
